page: Vultr voc-s-32c-256gb-5760s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute instance featuring 32 vCPUs powered by AMD EPYC Milan processors running at 2.0 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The server is configured with 256.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. Storage is bundled with a substantial 5760 GB local NVMe SSD, offering high capacity for data-intensive operations. The CPU allocation is shared, which represents a key architectural tradeoff for workloads requiring sustained, exclusive physical core performance.

Benchmark data indicates strong overall performance, particularly in memory operations and multi-threaded execution. The instance achieves top-tier status in large-block memory bandwidth, with read speeds of 515.15 GB/sec and write speeds of 253.87 GB/sec. Single-core CPU performance via stress-ng is also in the top tier, while multi-core CPU performance and PassMark CPU scores rank in the top 25%. For specialized tasks, the server demonstrates strong multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and highly efficient Redis SET operations, processing 70.76 million operations per second. Medium-model LLM text generation also registers in the top 25% tier.

Given its shared CPU model, this instance offers a cost-efficient alternative for memory-heavy and storage-intensive workloads that do not require dedicated CPU pinning. The combination of a 256.0 GB memory array and 5.76 TB of local NVMe storage makes it highly suitable for large-scale database hosting, in-memory caching systems, and data-processing pipelines. It is also well-suited for web serving and moderate language model inference tasks where high memory capacity and fast storage throughput are prioritized over dedicated compute guarantees.
description: Vultr voc-s-32c-256gb-5760s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute instance featuring 32 shared vCPUs on AMD EPYC Milan processors, 256.0 GB of RAM, and 5760 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Operating on the x86_64 architecture, this server delivers top-tier large-block memory bandwidth alongside strong multi-core CPU performance. While the shared CPU allocation presents a tradeoff for sustained compute-bound tasks, the high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU and massive local storage capacity provide excellent qualitative cost efficiency for resource-dense environments. It is highly suited for large-scale databases, memory-intensive caching layers like Redis, and data processing pipelines.
og_description: Vultr voc-s-32c-256gb-5760s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute instance with 32 AMD EPYC Milan vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 5760 GB NVMe storage, delivering top-tier memory bandwidth for database workloads.
meta_description: Vultr voc-s-32c-256gb-5760s-amd features 32 AMD EPYC Milan vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 5760 GB NVMe SSD storage for memory-intensive database workloads.
tagline: A high-capacity memory and NVMe storage instance designed for data-intensive workloads, database hosting, and large-scale caching.
bullet_points: 32 AMD EPYC Milan vCPUs with 256.0 GB of system memory
Bundled 5760 GB local NVMe SSD storage for high-capacity data needs
Top-tier large-block memory read and write bandwidth performance
Shared CPU allocation offering a cost-efficient profile for memory-dense tasks
Optimized for large-scale databases, Redis caching, and data processing
